channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Trump unveils $20 billion investment in American data centers
item.description: U.S. President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday announced a $20 billion new investment to build data centers across the United States. At a news conference at his home in Palm Beach, Florida, Trump said the investor is Emirati billionaire Hussain Sajwani, chairman of DAMAC properties.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zoox and Waymo are hoping Trump will ease barriers for self-driving cars
item.description: A group representing self-driving car companies on Tuesday called on the U.S. government to do more to speed the deployment of autonomous vehicles and remove barriers to adoption.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Jeff Bezos’ Amazon pays $40 million for Melania Trump documentary
item.description: The choice by Bezos's film and TV studio to pay such a sum to produce Melania Trump's 'vanity documentary' represents the latest public effort by the Amazon founder to "warm up" to Trump after years of feuding, media observers say.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Trump says Meta has 'come a long way' after it ends fact-checking program
item.description: President-elect Trump on Tuesday said that social media giant Meta has progressed as a company after it announced that it would eliminate its fact-checking program.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Biggest Nvidia takeaways from Jensen Huang's CES 2025 keynote
item.description: Nvidia’s stock is on the upswing after its CEO, Jensen Huang, unveiled a suite of new products, services and partnerships at CES 2025.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New labels will help people pick devices less at risk of hacking
item.description: Smart devices like baby monitors, fitness trackers and internet-connected appliances could soon carry labels certifying that they meet federal cybersecurity standards.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Think Oura and Samsung's smart rings look good? Ultrahuman just unveiled its Ultrahuman Rare 18K gold and platinum luxury smart rings at CES 2025
item.description: Oura and Samsung rival Ultrahuman has launched smart rings made from real 18K gold and platinum at CES.
item.dc:creator: matt.evans@futurenet.com (Matt Evans), TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia brings Blackwell to your desk - Project DIGITS mini PC is more like a mini supercomputer
item.description: Thought the Mac mini M4 Pro was powerful? Nvidia's new mini-supercomputer can handle 200b parameter LLMs.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Dolby Vision Filmmaker Mode is coming to more TVs in 2025 – here’s why that’s important
item.description: Dolby Vision Filmmaker Mode provides an easy way to get a director-approved HDR picture, and it's coming to TCL TVs.
item.dc:creator: al.griffin@futurenet.com (Al Griffin), TechRadar

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lost your contacts on your phone? Here's how to get them back
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides two methods to restore your contacts for iPhone and Android devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Try this technique to schedule deep work directly into your calendar
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. I’m starting this year with a focus on tactics, not just tools. Timeboxing is the tactic I rely on to plan my 100 weekly waking hours.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Uber and Lyft to offer driverless rideshare cars
item.description: Customers in Atlanta and Austin, Texas, will be able to hail one of these driverless rideshare cars via the Uber app.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, The Hill

item.title: Nvidia's new GPU series led an avalanche of entertainment-related announcements at CES
item.description: Nvidia unveiled its GeForce RTX 50 Series desktop and laptop GPUs — powered by its new Blackwell artificial intelligence chip — kicking off a string of entertainment-related AI announcements and discussions at the trade show, including several terse conversations about regulation of the budding tech
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Agentic AI: The top challenges and how to overcome them
item.description: As generative AI continues to surge in popularity, we are already seeing it evolve into the next generation of machine-learning-driven technology: agentic AI. With agentic AI, we are not just prompting models and receiving an answer in a simple one-step process.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft will invest $80B in AI data centers in fiscal 2025
item.description: Microsoft is on track to invest around $80 billion this fiscal year to build out AI-enabled data centers, company president Brad Smith said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg denies claims of building doomsday bunker in Hawaii
item.description: "I think it got blown out of proportion as if the whole ranch was some kind of doomsday bunker," Zuckerberg said.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Hacked Chrome extensions put 2.6 million users at risk of data leak
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are embedding dangerous code in Chrome extensions to steal your data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ruby completes switch to Prism parser
item.description: Ruby 3.4.0, and Ruby 3.4.1, updates to the longstanding dynamic, open source programming language, have arrived, with Prism now the default parser. Ruby 3.4.0 was released December 25, Christmas Day, along with an update, Ruby 3.4.1, which merely changed the version number of the release. Ruby 3.4.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Go teams struggle with coding standards – survey
item.description: Although developer sentiment toward the Go language remains very positive, teams face a challenge when it comes to maintaining consistent coding standards across Go code bases, according to Go developer survey results for the second half of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Confronting the growing drone threat
item.description: There is a solid rationale for prioritizing safe, secure and controlled RF cyber-based counter-UAS technology as decision makers consider future standards. The post Confronting the growing drone threat first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: IRS deploys AI tools to combat emerging tech’s role in new fraud schemes
item.description: AI tools are accelerating the development of deepfake schemes and disinformation campaigns that led to fraud The IRS is trying to keep up. The post IRS deploys AI tools to combat emerging tech’s role in new fraud schemes first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network
